---
pagetitle: "Architecture 2.0 - Designing AI-assisted loops for computing systems"
description: "A synthesis lecture and community hub for auditable AI-assisted loops in computer architecture, hardware/software co-design, and computing-system synthesis."
format:
  html:
    page-layout: custom
    toc: false
---

```{=html}
<div class="site-wrap">
<section class="site-hero hub-hero">
  <div>
    <div class="hub-kicker">Academic hub for AI-assisted computer architecture</div>
    <h1>Architecture&nbsp;2.0</h1>
    <p class="subtitle">
      Designing AI-assisted loops for computing systems.
    </p>

    <p class="site-lede">
    A professional home for the book, tool registry, reading list, workshops, and
    community infrastructure behind auditable AI-assisted loops for chips,
    accelerators, memory systems, simulators, compilers, benchmarks, tools, and
    evidence workflows. As AI enters these architecture loops, the pressing
    question is no longer whether a model can propose a design, but whether we
    can trust the loop that produced it.
    </p>

    <nav class="site-actions" aria-label="Primary">
      <a class="site-action" href="/book/index.html">Read the book <span class="arr">&rarr;</span></a>
      <a class="site-action secondary" href="/readings.html">Browse readings <span class="arr">&rarr;</span></a>
      <a class="site-action tertiary" href="/workshops.html">Find workshops</a>
    </nav>
  </div>

  <div class="hub-panel" role="complementary" aria-label="Architecture 2.0 resources">
    <div class="hub-panel-header">
      <p class="hub-panel-title">Featured resources</p>
      <span class="hub-status">Work in progress</span>
    </div>
    <div class="hub-resource-list">
      <a class="hub-resource" href="/book/index.html">
        <span class="resource-icon">BK</span>
        <span><strong>Synthesis lecture</strong><span>Design loops, evidence, rejection, and architect-owned judgment.</span></span>
        <span class="resource-arrow">&rarr;</span>
      </a>
      <a class="hub-resource" href="/tools/index.html">
        <span class="resource-icon">TL</span>
        <span><strong>Tool registry</strong><span>Simulators, proxy models, verification harnesses, and benchmarks.</span></span>
        <span class="resource-arrow">&rarr;</span>
      </a>
      <a class="hub-resource" href="/readings.html">
        <span class="resource-icon">RD</span>
        <span><strong>Reading list</strong><span>Papers, SIGARCH posts, talks, and workshop writeups.</span></span>
        <span class="resource-arrow">&rarr;</span>
      </a>
      <a class="hub-resource" href="/workshops.html">
        <span class="resource-icon">WS</span>
        <span><strong>Workshop tracker</strong><span>Community venues where Architecture 2.0 work can be shared.</span></span>
        <span class="resource-arrow">&rarr;</span>
      </a>
      <a class="hub-resource" href="/huggingface.html">
        <span class="resource-icon">HF</span>
        <span><strong>🤗 Hugging Face</strong><span>Coming-soon artifact hub for datasets, models, examples, and credit-preserving shared resources.</span></span>
        <span class="resource-arrow">&rarr;</span>
      </a>
    </div>
  </div>
</section>

<section class="site-section">
  <div class="section-heading">
    <h2>Start here</h2>
  </div>
  <div class="resource-grid">
    <a class="resource-card" href="/book/index.html">
      <span class="site-pill">Book</span>
      <h3>The book</h3>
      <p>A synthesis lecture under preparation, defining the state, action, rejection, and evidence a trustworthy computing-system design loop requires.</p>
      <span class="resource-card-footer">Read online</span>
    </a>
    <a class="resource-card" href="/tools/index.html">
      <span class="site-pill">Tools</span>
      <h3>The tool registry</h3>
      <p>A community-maintained registry of simulators, proxy models, verification harnesses, benchmarks, and data representations.</p>
      <span class="resource-card-footer">Browse tools</span>
    </a>
    <a class="resource-card" href="/readings.html">
      <span class="site-pill">Readings</span>
      <h3>The reading list</h3>
      <p>A curated path through papers, SIGARCH posts, talks, datasets, and workshop writeups around Architecture 2.0.</p>
      <span class="resource-card-footer">Read papers and posts</span>
    </a>
    <a class="resource-card" href="/workshops.html">
      <span class="site-pill">Workshops</span>
      <h3>The workshop tracker</h3>
      <p>A shared list of relevant workshops where people can submit, present, and find related community activity.</p>
      <span class="resource-card-footer">See venues</span>
    </a>
    <a class="resource-card" href="/huggingface.html">
      <span class="site-pill">Artifacts</span>
      <h3>The artifact hub</h3>
      <p>A coming-soon public home for datasets, models, examples, and shared resources that preserves author and institution credit.</p>
      <span class="resource-card-footer">See plan</span>
    </a>
  </div>
</section>

<section class="site-section">
  <div class="section-heading">
    <h2>How the hub builds momentum</h2>
    <p>The site should make community work visible without turning every contribution into a maintenance burden.</p>
  </div>
  <div class="pathway-list">
    <div class="pathway-step">
      <span>01</span>
      <h3>Read the framework</h3>
      <p>The book gives the vocabulary for state, action, rejection, evidence, and human commitment.</p>
    </div>
    <div class="pathway-step">
      <span>02</span>
      <h3>Find tools and venues</h3>
      <p>The registries point newcomers to papers, working software, benchmarks, workshops, and artifact spaces.</p>
    </div>
    <div class="pathway-step">
      <span>03</span>
      <h3>Submit visible work</h3>
      <p>Structured GitHub templates turn a submission into a reviewable PR and a public project card.</p>
    </div>
    <div class="pathway-step">
      <span>04</span>
      <h3>Build in the open</h3>
      <p>Discussions, workshops, and shared artifacts make the field easier to join and easier to cite.</p>
    </div>
  </div>
</section>

</div>
```
